Table 10. Per-client test accuracy (%) under intra-domain settings with 3 clients. Left: Reward-Margin Partition (IMDB). Right: Language-Based Partition (Code-Vulnerability-Security). Results are mean ± std over 5 runs.

Table 1. Test accuracy (%) under intra-domain settings with 3, 10, or 11 clients and cross-domain settings with distinct datasets per client (3 clients). Code-V.: Code-Vulnerability-Security, UltraF.: UltraFeedback. Bold and underline denote the best and second-best results.
Table 2. Test accuracy (%) under cross-domain settings with TinyLlama-1.1B on distinct datasets per client (3 clients).

Figure 2. Overview of FedPDPO. Each client fine-tunes and shares a LoRA adapter (A,B) on a frozen backbone W 0 while locally training personalized modules: a bottleneck adapter M , local LLM and reward heads (h1,h2) with an effective PDPO training strategy.

Figure 5. An example preference triple from the IMDB dataset. The prompt is a movie review prefix, and the model must learn to prefer the chosen response (positive sentiment, blue) over the rejected response (negative sentiment, red).
Figure 8. Average accuracy (%) varies with reward head dimension across five benchmark settings.
Figure 7. Average accuracy (%) varies with LoRA rank across five benchmark settings.
Figure 6. Distribution of reward margins across the IMDB dataset and their non-IID partition into three subsets (easy, medium, hard).
